skill.name: ascii-visualizer
skill.description: Creates ASCII diagrams for architecture, workflows, implementation plans, comparisons, progress summaries, and file structures.
skill.summary: Turn complex technical ideas into clear ASCII diagrams and visual explanations.
skill.author: ArieGoldkin

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, weak cryptography, reconnaissance, and sensitive file patterns, but review found these are false positives in Markdown examples and ASCII diagram text. No executable scripts, network calls, filesystem access, credential handling, or prompt injection attempts were found in the reviewed files. The only minor concern is broad behavioral wording that encourages frequent ASCII use, which is not a security blocker.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fences Flagged as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged triple backticks as Ruby or shell execution. The locations are Markdown code fences that display ASCII examples and are not executable commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is inside Markdown fences and consists of visual diagrams and sample text. I found no shell command invocation, script file, or execution instruction at these locations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Security Keywords in Documentation Text
low_findings.description: Weak cryptography, Windows SAM, and reconnaissance alerts map to architecture labels, prose, dates, metrics, and skill names. The reviewed lines do not reference cryptographic APIs, SAM database access, or host reconnaissance commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown documentation and ASCII diagram content, not operational code. There is no evidence of cryptographic implementation, Windows SAM access, or system reconnaissance behavior.

low_findings.title: Minor Behavioral Overreach in Skill Guidance
low_findings.description: The skill says the user explicitly loves ASCII art and asks the assistant to use it liberally. This may over-apply the skill, but it does not create a direct security risk.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The wording is clearly present and can influence response style. The confidence is lower for security impact because it affects presentation behavior rather than access to data or tools.

content.user_title: Create ASCII Diagrams for Complex Ideas
content.value_statement: Complex plans and architectures are hard to scan in plain text. This skill turns them into compact ASCII diagrams that work well in terminals, issues, docs, and chat.
actual_capabilities: Creates box diagrams for components, systems, and data flow.
Formats file trees with labels for folders, files, and responsibilities.
Builds workflow diagrams that show steps, decisions, and handoffs.
Creates before-and-after comparisons for plans, metrics, and tradeoffs.
Adds progress bars and status markers for implementation summaries.
Uses consistent box-drawing characters for readable terminal output.
limitations: Does not generate images, SVG files, or interactive diagrams.
Does not validate architecture claims against a real repository by itself.
Large diagrams may wrap in narrow chat windows or mobile screens.
Best results require concise labels and clear relationships.

use_cases.title: Explain a System Architecture
use_cases.description: Show services, modules, data flow, and ownership boundaries in a text-only diagram.

use_cases.title: Plan an Implementation
use_cases.description: Convert a multi-step delivery plan into phases, dependencies, and completion markers.

use_cases.title: Document a Repository Structure
use_cases.description: Create a readable file tree that explains folder purpose and important entry points.

prompt_templates.title: Visualize a Simple Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an ASCII flow diagram for this process: [describe the steps]. Keep labels short and readable.

prompt_templates.title: Map a Repository
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an ASCII file tree for this repository structure: [paste folders]. Add short purpose notes for key directories.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Two Options
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an ASCII comparison between [option A] and [option B]. Include tradeoffs, risks, and expected impact.

prompt_templates.title: Design an Architecture Brief
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a layered ASCII architecture diagram for [system]. Show clients, services, data stores, integrations, and critical data paths.

output_examples.input: Show the flow from user request to API response.
output_examples.output: User Request -> Web App -> API Route -> Service Layer -> Database -> Response. The skill presents this as aligned boxes with arrows and short labels.

output_examples.input: Compare the current folder structure with the proposed structure.
output_examples.output: A compact before-and-after ASCII table showing folder count, ownership clarity, migration effort, and expected maintenance impact.

output_examples.input: Document the main parts of a results dashboard.
output_examples.output: A file-tree style diagram with app routes, feature modules, shared components, hooks, utilities, and notes for each major folder.

best_practices: Keep labels short so diagrams fit within common terminal widths.
Ask for the source structure or process steps before creating detailed diagrams.
Use one diagram per concept when the system has many layers.
anti_patterns: Do not use ASCII diagrams when a normal paragraph would be clearer.
Do not present invented architecture details as verified facts.
Do not build very wide diagrams that require horizontal scrolling.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates text-based ASCII diagrams for architecture, workflows, plans, comparisons, progress, and file structures.

faq.question: Does it run commands or inspect my files?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed skill only provides formatting guidance and examples. It does not include executable scripts.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The marketplace metadata lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is it suitable for technical documentation?
faq.answer: Yes. It is useful for README files, implementation notes, design reviews, and issue comments.

faq.question: Can it replace a diagramming tool?
faq.answer: No. It is best for lightweight text diagrams, not polished graphics or interactive visuals.

faq.question: How can I get better output?
faq.answer: Provide the key entities, relationships, sequence, and any width limits before asking for the diagram.
